marketState: neutral with a bearish medium-term skew
summary: Front-month gas ($2.94) sits almost exactly at modeled fair value ($2.917 now, rising to $3.008 by +21D) across every horizon, so there is no meaningful mispricing edge to trade right now. Seasonality is running -10.3pp below its typical-year path with a bearish-skewed 4-week probability profile (31% chance higher, typical move -3.1%), though reliability is flagged low (50pp spread). Storage is the clearest directional signal: a +72 Bcf bearish surprise build left inventories +184 Bcf above the 5-year average, arguing for continued downside pressure into the shoulder season. Weather is a modest near-term offset - 1-7D cooling demand is running +6% above the 5-year norm (rangePos 0.62), which should provide some price support over the next week or two, but the 8-16D read fades toward neutral (+2.5%, rangePos 0.55). Technicals are bearish (death-cross EMA structure) on nearly every timeframe from 15m out to 1w, but 4h/1d RSI (24.9/35.6) and a daily close below the lower Bollinger band flag the move as stretched, raising bounce risk for short-term shorts.

longTerm.entry: $2.95 - $3.05 (scale in on strength toward fair value)
longTerm.sizing: 1R approx 1% account risk (~$0.20/MMBtu risk per unit)
longTerm.rationale: Storage sits +184 Bcf above the 5-year average after a +72 Bcf bearish surprise build, and seasonality shows price already -10.3pp below its typical-year path with a bearish-skewed 4-week outlook (31% probability higher, typical move -3.1%). COT shows managed money net short but only at the 35th percentile, leaving room to add rather than signaling a crowded/squeeze-prone short.
longTerm.invalidation: A sustained run of larger-than-normal storage draws that closes the 5y surplus, or 8-16D weather demand breaking meaningfully above normal (>+10% vs 5y), would invalidate the bearish case.

shortTerm.rationale: Front price is trading right on top of fair value ($2.94 vs $2.917, spread +$0.023, status FAIR) so there is no pricing edge, and signals conflict: modestly above-normal near-term cooling demand (+6% vs 5y) and oversold 4h/1d RSI (24.9/35.6) with price below the lower daily Bollinger band argue for a bounce, while the daily/weekly trend and EMA structure remain bearish (50<200 on every timeframe).
shortTerm.trigger: Wait for either a daily RSI reclaim above 40 with MACD histogram turning positive (bounce/long setup) or a confirmed daily close below $2.80 with bearish EMA/MACD alignment (breakdown/short setup) before committing capital.
